novel_features.name: Setlist prediction
novel_features.description: Generate a likely setlist for an upcoming show using recency-weighted per-song probability from the artist's recent tour.
novel_features.rationale: Requires every recent setlist for one artist in a local store; the API returns one page at a time and rate-limits live calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent or fan asks 'what will they play tonight', this answers it with confidence-ranked output instead of forcing a manual scrape of the last ten setlists.

novel_features.name: Per-song stats
novel_features.description: For one song: total plays, first/last date, longest gap, average set position, percentage of shows that included it.
novel_features.rationale: Needs the full song history table joined to setlists; a single API call can only return one setlist at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers the canonical fan question ('when was the last time they played X?') without burning 50 API calls.

novel_features.name: Overdue songs
novel_features.description: Rank an artist's songs by shows-since-last-played to surface what is most due to return.
novel_features.rationale: Requires global song frequency across all of the artist's recent shows in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent predict the 'wildcard' slot of a setlist without re-fetching the artist's whole tour.

novel_features.name: Tour shape
novel_features.description: Median set length, encore frequency, song-position histogram, top openers and closers for one tour.
novel_features.rationale: Tour-wide aggregates only emerge after joining setlists+sets+songs locally; the API exposes only per-setlist views.
novel_features.example: setlist-fm-pp-cli tour shape 'Radiohead' --tour 'The Tour of the OK Computer' --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Gives an agent a one-shot summary of how an artist is structuring a tour, replacing dozens of read calls.

novel_features.name: Tour comparison
novel_features.description: Overlap percent, dropped songs, debuts, and set-position shifts between two named tours of one artist.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both tours fully sync'd to the local store; no API endpoint returns this directly.
novel_features.example: setlist-fm-pp-cli compare 'Phoenix' --tour 'Ti Amo Tour' --tour 'Alpha Zulu Tour' --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces tour evolution in one call for review/journalism workflows.

novel_features.name: Encore intelligence
novel_features.description: Top encore openers, top encore closers, percent of shows that had an encore at all.
novel_features.rationale: Requires identifying encore sets across many setlists; the API does not aggregate.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents answer 'what do they always close with?' offline.

novel_features.name: Cover finder
novel_features.description: All cover songs an artist has played live, ranked by frequency with the original artist.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the per-song cover flag across thousands of setlists; no single API call returns this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces fan-discovery moments (rare covers, recurring covers) for journalism or curation.

novel_features.name: Setlist diff
novel_features.description: Side-by-side song diff of two setlist IDs.
novel_features.rationale: API only returns one setlist at a time; diffing requires both in memory simultaneously.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents answer 'what changed between these two shows' without parsing two responses by hand.

novel_features.name: Song gap
novel_features.description: Biggest dry spells for one song and when the comeback happened.
novel_features.rationale: Computed from local song history; the API has no concept of song-level gaps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents narrate band history without re-fetching the full setlist history.

novel_features.name: Rare debuts
novel_features.description: Songs an artist has played exactly once live.
novel_features.rationale: Needs the full song count distribution; only computable from the local aggregate.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces one-off oddities (one-time covers, abandoned originals) for rare-finding workflows.

novel_features.name: Attended dashboard
novel_features.description: Total shows, unique artists/songs/venues/cities, biggest streak, longest gap, decade breakdown for a user.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the user's full attended history with the songs table; the API exposes only paginated raw lists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Delivers a one-shot collector dashboard that the website does not produce.

novel_features.name: Bingo card
novel_features.description: Printable bingo card of N most-likely-to-play songs for an upcoming show.
novel_features.rationale: Derived from the same probability model as predict; cannot be assembled from API output alone.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Fan-festival use case; gives a delightful tangible output the API was never going to ship.

novel_features.name: Venue loyalty
novel_features.description: Top venues an artist plays at, by frequency. Detects 'home venue' patterns.
novel_features.rationale: Joins all of an artist's setlists with venues; the API only returns venues one at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents reason about artist-venue affinity without dozens of API calls.

novel_features.name: Since-cursor digest
novel_features.description: Setlist updates since a given timestamp; pair with sync for delta refresh.
novel_features.rationale: Cursor-driven incremental sync requires local state; not native to the API.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets a daily-digest agent stay current without re-syncing the full history.

novel_features.name: Spotify playlist export
novel_features.description: Generate a Spotify playlist from an artist's most recent setlist (or merged last N setlists) — output as CSV, M3U, or Spotify-search URIs, or use the Spotify Web API to create the playlist directly.
novel_features.rationale: Reads from the local Setlist.fm store and calls Spotify's real API; not possible from a pure Setlist.fm SDK because no wrapper persists data or talks to Spotify.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns a concert log into something an agent or user can listen to in one command — the bridge between data and ear that no wrapper builds.

narrative.headline: Every Setlist.fm endpoint, plus offline analytics no API call can return — tour shape, song frequency, what's overdue, setlist prediction.
narrative.value_prop: Setlist.fm rate-limits to 2 requests per second and 1,440 per day, which makes any real analytics workflow impossible against the live API. This CLI syncs an artist's full setlist history to a local SQLite store once, then runs every transcendence query — predict, overdue, tour shape, song gap, covers, attended stats — instantly and offline. Six SDK wrappers exist across five languages; none of them store anything. This one does.
narrative.auth_narrative: Get a free API key at https://www.setlist.fm/settings/api. The CLI looks for SETLISTFM_API_KEY in the environment and falls back to SETLIST_FM_API_KEY for compatibility with existing Python and JavaScript tooling. Run `setlist-fm-pp-cli auth set-token <key>` to persist it in the config file. All requests automatically throttle to 2 RPS and surface 429 responses with a backoff hint.

quickstart.comment: Persist your API key once; every later command reads it from the config.
Get the MusicBrainz ID without copy-pasting from the website.
Pull the last few pages of setlists into the local SQLite store under your daily-request budget.
Run the first transcendence query: which songs are most due for a comeback.
Generate a predicted setlist for the next show from the recent tour.

troubleshoots.fix: Your API key is missing or invalid. Run `setlist-fm-pp-cli doctor` to confirm SETLISTFM_API_KEY is loaded.
You exceeded 2 req/sec or 1440/day. The client already throttles, but parallel runs will trip it. Wait, then re-run; consider `sync --max-pages N` to budget.
These commands read from the local store. Run `setlist-fm-pp-cli sync --artist <name>` first.
Increase `--last N` (default 10) so more shows feed the probability model; sync more pages first.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI whenever an agent or user needs to reason across many of an artist's setlists at once — predicting a show, finding an overdue song, counting how often a cover is played, comparing two tours, or building a dashboard of a user's attended history. Use the raw `search`/`get` commands for one-shot lookups; use the transcendence commands (predict, overdue, tour shape, compare, attended stats) when you need an aggregate the API cannot return in one call. The 2-RPS rate limit makes this CLI strictly faster than any live-API wrapper for repeated questions.

recipes.title: Predict tonight's setlist
recipes.explanation: Sync the recent tour into the store, then output a ranked setlist prediction with only the three fields an agent needs — drops a 30 KB response to ~2 KB.

recipes.title: What's overdue for a comeback?
recipes.explanation: Lists the ten songs Phish has gone the longest without playing, ordered by show-count gap.

recipes.title: Compare two tours
recipes.command: setlist-fm-pp-cli compare 'Phoenix' --tour 'Ti Amo Tour' --tour 'Alpha Zulu Tour' --agent
recipes.explanation: Shows song overlap, dropped songs, debuts, and set-position shifts between two named tours.

recipes.title: Build a fan bingo card
recipes.explanation: Generates a printable 5×5 card of the most-likely-to-play songs for the next show.

recipes.title: My concert dashboard
recipes.explanation: Returns total shows, unique artists/songs/venues, biggest streak, longest gap — the dashboard the website doesn't render.

trigger_phrases: how often does X play Y
